untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of dynamic trendline/parallel channel geometry (slope, width, breach confirmation across N bars), channel boundary tracking, and margin-based threshold comparisons. These require multi-bar pattern recognition and geometric calculations not available in the allowed indicator set. Pivot points used as proxy for support/resistance level, but true parallel channel dynamics cannot be expressed.
translation_notes: This signal fundamentally requires: (1) Real-time trendline/channel drawing and tracking, (2) Slope angle detection, (3) Width tolerance measurement, (4) Multi-candle sequential validation (breach + N-bar confirmation), (5) Margin-based breach threshold logic. The allowed indicators lack structural pattern recognition. Used pivot (S1/R1 equivalent) as best-effort substitute for sloping support/resistance, and volume confirmation for reversal strength, but this loses the critical channel geometry aspect of the original rule.
